questions.q: Which author, known for psychological thrillers like 'The Housemaid', recently revealed their real name is Sara Cohen and that they are also a doctor?
questions.correctText: Freida McFadden is the pen name of Sara Cohen, a doctor who writes bestselling thrillers like 'The Housemaid'.

questions.q: Amazon is ending support for Kindle devices released in which year or earlier, starting May 20, 2026?
questions.correctText: Starting May 20, 2026, Amazon will end support for Kindle devices released in 2012 or earlier.

questions.q: Which author declined the $175,000 Windham-Campbell Prize due to promotional requirements?
questions.correctText: Helen DeWitt turned down the $175,000 Windham-Campbell Prize because she was unable to fulfill the required promotional activities.

questions.q: According to some accounts, which magazine, known for its adult content, also published significant literary works and well-paid short stories, contributing to literacy for some readers?
questions.correctText: Playboy magazine, despite its reputation, published significant literary works, including short stories that paid very well, and is credited by some with fostering literacy.

questions.q: The discussion around AI-generated novels often raises concerns about copyright, as AI-generated works currently cannot be copyrighted in the US due to the requirement of human authorship. What is a potential impact of AI novels on the self-publishing market?
options: They will increase the value of traditionally published books.
They may devalue self-published books and create a stigma.
They will lead to greater demand for human-written novels.
They will be easily identifiable and filtered out by platforms like Amazon KDP.
questions.correctText: There is concern that the proliferation of AI-generated novels could lower the bar for self-publishing and bring back negative stigmas associated with self-published books being of lower quality.
